name: French Guiana (Guyane / Guyane française)
summary: French Guiana is a South American civil-law French overseas department and region (Département et région d'outre-mer / DROM) — structurally distinctive globally as the only South American French DROM and the location of the European Space Agency's Guiana Space Centre (Kourou). Population is composed of Creole, Amerindian (multiple indigenous nations including Kalina, Wayampi, Teko, Wayana, Lokono, Pahikwene), Maroon (Bushinengue including Aluku, Ndjuka, Saramaka, Paramaka), Brazilian, Haitian, and metropolitan French populations. Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code applied directly as in metropolitan France, with limited Amerindian customary-status accommodations under the 2007 Article 75 framework. Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387. The Court of Appeal of Cayenne is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Tribunal Judiciaire de Cayenne. Cross-border family-law coordination with neighboring Brazil and Suriname operates through standard France-Brazil/Suriname arrangements. Psychology profession is regulated through the French Republic professional framework as applicable in French Guiana. French Guiana is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the French interest-of-the-child standard. French Guiana is a Hague Convention 1980 party via French Republic territorial extension effective 1 December 1983.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in French Guiana)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applied directly in French Guiana as in metropolitan France. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution Article 75
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional Article providing for civil status under local customary-status (statut civil de droit local) for certain Amerindian populations in French Guiana.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Cayenne (Cour d'appel de Cayenne)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from French Guiana.

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from French Guiana.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in French Guiana)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in French Guiana.

anonymisation_convention: Guianan family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: French colonisation begins + Cayenne foundation framework
key_developments.description: French colonisation of Guiana commenced 1664 with Cayenne foundation. Substantively distinctive South American French-colonial framework persisting through subsequent centuries affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework including Amerindian (Kalina, Wayampi, Teko, Wayana, Lokono, Pahikwene) population and Maroon (Aluku, Ndjuka, Saramaka, Paramaka) populations.

key_developments.title: Devil's Island penal-colony 1852-1953 + bagne-distinctive framework
key_developments.description: French Guiana penal-colony (bagne / Devil's Island) 1852-1953 — substantively distinctive globally-significant penal-colony framework hosting ~70,000 prisoners over 100 years including Alfred Dreyfus 1895-1899. Substantive demographic and historical-significance framework affecting French Guiana's contemporary positioning.

key_developments.title: Departmental integration + DOM status + post-WWII framework
key_developments.description: French Guiana achieved departmental status (Département d'outre-mer / DOM) 19 March 1946 (Loi 46-451) — among the first French overseas DOMs alongside Martinique, Guadeloupe, Réunion. Substantively distinctive globally only-South-American-French-DROM framework — only EU and French presence in continental South America.

key_developments.title: Guiana Space Centre (Kourou) opening + ESA-EU space-framework
key_developments.description: Guiana Space Centre (Centre Spatial Guyanais / CSG) opened 14 April 1964 — substantively distinctive globally European Space Agency major space launch facility. Substantive space-economy framework affecting French Guiana's geopolitical positioning and family-law-cross-cutting-areas including expat-family contexts.

key_developments.title: EU integration + Outermost Region (OMR) framework
key_developments.description: French Guiana entered European Economic Community 1973 as part of France. Subsequent Outermost Region (région ultrapériphérique / OMR) status under TFEU Article 349 — substantively distinctive EU-South-American-Member-State-territory framework. Foundational EU-treaty framework affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with neighbouring Brazil and Suriname.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by France to French Guiana effective 1 December 1983 — establishing South American Hague Convention jurisdiction framework within French Republic territorial framework.

key_developments.title: France ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + French Guiana extension
key_developments.description: France ratified the UNCRC on 7 August 1990 with extension to French Guiana — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within French-civil-law DROM framework applicable to French Guiana with Amerindian customary-status accommodations.

key_developments.title: Constitution Article 75 framework + Amerindian customary-status consolidation
key_developments.description: Constitution Article 75 framework consolidated 2007 — substantively significant Amerindian customary-status (statut civil de droit local) framework for certain Amerindian populations in French Guiana. Substantively distinctive recognised-indigenous-personal-status framework within French Republic affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Movement 500 Frères + 2017 social-crisis + 500-million-euro-package
key_developments.description: Movement 500 Frères (500 Brothers) social-crisis March-April 2017 substantively disrupting French Guiana political-institutional framework — €1.085 billion accord-de-Guyane settlement. Substantive socio-economic crisis framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: CTG framework + Centre spatial guyanais + Brazil/Suriname cross-border framework
key_developments.description: Collectivité territoriale de Guyane (CTG) framework continuing through 2023 under single-territorial-collectivity framework. Centre spatial guyanais (CSG) framework substantively significant institutional context. Brazil/Suriname cross-border family-law adjudication framework substantively significant for cross-border family-law matters. Substantively significant South American French DROM institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour d'appel de Cayenne + Cour de Cassation — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Amerindian customary-status framework
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Cayenne and French Court of Cassation continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 framework for civil-status persons and Constitution Article 75 framework for Amerindian customary-status persons in custody disputes. Substantive analysis within French-civil-law DROM + Amerindian-customary-status + multi-ethnic-population framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: French Guiana operates a French-civil-law framework applied as in metropolitan France — places French Guiana in the French DROM cluster.
Only South American French DROM is structurally distinctive globally — only EU and French presence in continental South America.
Multi-ethnic population composition (Creole + Amerindian multiple nations + Maroon + Brazilian + Haitian + metropolitan French) is structurally distinctive within French overseas territory cluster.
Amerindian customary-status accommodations under Constitution Article 75 framework are structurally distinctive — recognised indigenous-personal-status framework within French Republic.
Guiana Space Centre (Kourou) European Space Agency facility is structurally distinctive globally — only sovereign state with EU/ESA major space launch facility on its territory.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via French Republic territorial extension reflects DROM Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: French Guiana jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full French-colonisation-to-contemporary trajectory: 1664-French-colonisation-+-Cayenne + 1852-Devil's-Island-penal-colony + 1946-Departmental-integration + 1964-Guiana-Space-Centre-Kourou + 1973-EU-integration-+-Outermost-Region + 1983-Hague-Convention-1980-territorial-extension + 1990-France-UNCRC-+-French-Guiana-extension + 2007-Constitution-Article-75-Amerindian-customary-status + 2017-Movement-500-Frères-+-social-crisis + 2024-Cour-d'appel-de-Cayenne-+-Cour-de-Cassation.

Civil-law South American French DROM (French Civil Code + Constitution Article 75 Amerindian customary-status framework + multi-ethnic population + Guiana Space Centre + Hague via French Republic territorial extension 1983). Only South American French DROM globally + only EU presence in continental South America.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 371-387 for civil-status persons + Constitution Article 75 for Amerindian customary-status persons without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins South-American + civil-law + French-DROM-cluster + only-South-American-French-DROM-globally-distinctive + only-EU-and-French-presence-in-continental-South-America + Amerindian-multiple-nations-Kalina-Wayampi-Teko-Wayana-Lokono-Pahikwene + Maroon-Bushinengue-Aluku-Ndjuka-Saramaka-Paramaka + Devil's-Island-penal-colony-1852-1953-distinctive + Guiana-Space-Centre-Kourou-1964-ESA-only-EU-major-space-launch-distinctive + Movement-500-Frères-2017-1.085-billion-accord-de-Guyane + EU-Outermost-Region-TFEU-Article-349 + Hague-via-French-Republic-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
